forecast-period.text: A cold front will pass over the southeast this morning followed by a high-pressure system building south of the state today. The high will then move east on Wednesday allowing an upper trough to bring instability to southern parts of the state during the day, persisting into Thursday. The upper trough will precede a vigorous cold front that is forecast to move over South Australia Thursday afternoon. A cooler southwest to southerly flow will gradually moderate as a stronger high-pressure system moves in from the west from Friday to be south of the state on Sunday.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones.

Light showers about the southern agricultural area, mainly about windward coasts and ranges during the morning, mostly clearing during the afternoon. Middle level cloud, isolated showers and possible thunderstorms about the Nullarbor Plain, with showers extending across the west during the day, possibly reaching central and eastern parts of the northern agricultural area or south of the Pastoral districts in the evening. Mostly sunny in the far north. Maximum temperatures below average about the agricultural area, grading to above average in the north. Southwest to southeasterly winds tending northeast to northwesterly in the north and west later in the day.

Areas of high cloud, showers and isolated thunderstorms extending from the west over the agricultural area and far south of the pastoral districts. Maximum temperatures slightly below average about the southeast, grading to above average in the north and west. Southeast to northeasterly winds at first, tending northeast to northwesterly throughout. Light to moderate local afternoon sea breezes in the west.

Showers and isolated thunderstorms extending about the agricultural area and south of the pastoral districts, increasing during the afternoon and evening. Maximum temperatures above average to well above average with freshening north to northwesterly winds, grading to below average about western coastal districts following a fresh west to southwesterly change over western and southern parts, possibly reaching the northeast later.

text.p: Showers about the southern agricultural area with isolated showers reaching the northern agricultural area and the West Coast. Maximum temperatures below average grading to slightly above average in the far northeast. Fresh to strong and gusty west to southwesterly winds moderating across the north and west during the afternoon.

Cumulative rainfall totals to the end of Friday: 2 to 10 mm across the agricultural area and the west, lowering to less than 2 mm about the south of the Pastoral districts. Local falls of 10 to 40 mm are possible across parts of the southern agricultural area and Nullarbor Plain, mostly with thunderstorms.
